▶
Concurrent Processing
▶
Prefix Cache
Speeds up repeated conversations by remembering previous prompts. Makes follow-up messages much faster (lower time-to-first-token).
Enable Prefix Cache
?
Legacy Entry-Count Cache
?
Cache Memory Limit and Cache Memory % only apply to memory-aware non-paged prefix cache. With paged cache on, use Max Cache Blocks for L1 RAM capacity and Block Cache Max for L2 disk capacity.
Cache Memory Limit (MB)
?
Auto-detect
Cache Memory %
?
Cache TTL has no effect when paged cache is enabled — paged cache uses block-count LRU eviction instead. To control paged cache size, adjust 'Max Cache Blocks' in the Paged KV Cache section below. To use time-based TTL, disable 'Use Paged KV Cache' in the Paged KV Cache section.
Cache TTL (minutes)
?
No expiration
▶
Paged KV Cache
Reduces memory waste by splitting the KV cache into small blocks instead of one big chunk. Lets the server handle longer conversations without running out of RAM.
Paged cache and legacy Disk Cache cannot run simultaneously. Enabling paged cache will auto-disable legacy Disk Cache. For persistent caching with paged cache, use 'Block Disk Cache (L2)' below instead.
Hybrid/Mamba cache models require paged cache while prefix cache is enabled so KV blocks an
